codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of dynamic param extraction replacing the hardcoded extract_locked_params_for_cascade(). The recursive resolution approach in _resolve_locked_recursive and _resolve_sweep_recursive handles the full complexity of the config model (per-TF overrides, {locked: value} wrappers, nested dicts, metadata stripping). The Cartesian product generation in extract_sweep_combos correctly uses deepcopy of locked baseline as template and preserves wrapper structures during value injection. The CascadeEngine round-trip test (9784 detections) provides very strong equivalence validation. The YAML config fix adding 1m values for liquidity_sweep was necessary and correctly done. Two non-blocking issues found: dead code (unused helper functions and imports) and a missing test for invalid mode parameter.

issues.description: Dead code: _load_raw_yaml() is defined but never called anywhere in the module or codebase. The 'yaml' and 'Path' imports (lines 24-25) are only needed by these dead functions. Similarly _get_raw_primitive_data() at line 82 is unused. These appear to be leftover from an earlier approach that read raw YAML. Should be removed to keep the module clean.

Missing test for invalid mode parameter. The implementation has explicit validation at line 122 raising ParamExtractionError for invalid modes, but no test covers this path. A simple test like `extract_params(config, 'fvg', mode='invalid')` asserting ParamExtractionError would complete the error-handling coverage.

The evaluation package __init__.py exports EvaluationRunner but not the param extraction functions (extract_params, extract_sweep_combos, ParamExtractionError). Consumers must use the full module path. This is consistent with the runner.py importing directly, but worth noting for API discoverability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The param_extraction module contains two dead helper functions (_load_raw_yaml and _get_raw_primitive_data) and their associated imports (yaml, Path). AGENTS.md does not mention a convention about avoiding dead code or requiring clean imports. Not a major gap, but could be worth a brief note in coding conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/ra/evaluation/param_extraction.py lines 24-25 (imports yaml, Path), lines 75-101 (two functions defined, zero call sites found via grep)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed procedure correctly. Skill says to write failing tests first, study specs, implement, run full suite. Handoff confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations. No gaps observed.

summary: PASS. The param-extraction-refactor feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (61 tests covering all 12 VAL-PARAM assertions). The core equivalence guarantee is strongly validated by both parametrized locked extraction tests across all 14 primitives and the CascadeEngine round-trip test confirming identical 9784 detections. Two non-blocking issues found: (1) dead code — two unused helper functions and their imports should be cleaned up, (2) missing test for the invalid-mode error path. No blocking issues, no security concerns, no performance problems. The YAML config fix for liquidity_sweep 1m values was necessary and correctly done.
